M-053.NAME: mtproxy-secret-sync
M-053.owns: Per-user official MTProxy secret lifecycle, active-secret manifest generation, runtime apply/revoke/reissue, startup replay, and redacted sync health.
M-053.purpose: Keep KrotPN assignment state and the official MTProxy runtime secret list aligned without exposing raw secret material outside owner-only payloads.

exports.fn-derive-official-secret: Derive or materialize one 16-byte official MTProxy secret for a verified user's canonical assignment.
exports.fn-build-secure-official-link: Build the owner-only Telegram link with secret=dd<hex-secret>.
exports.fn-render-secret-manifest: Render active official secrets into an atomic runtime manifest without logging secret values.
exports.fn-apply-secret-manifest: Apply the manifest to the official runtime and report degraded state when runtime apply fails.
exports.fn-revoke-user-secret: Remove exactly one user's active secret from the manifest and runtime after admin revoke.
exports.fn-reissue-user-secret: Rotate exactly one user's secret and preserve unrelated active assignments.
exports.task-secret-replay: Replay active assignments after backend, runtime, or restored-server startup.

lifecycle-contract.state-1: create: verified email activation creates a canonical assignment and official credential mode before owner payload is returned.
lifecycle-contract.state-2: apply: active assignment secrets are synced to M-052 before the user sees an active proxy status.
lifecycle-contract.state-3: replay: backend/runtime startup reconciles all active assignments into the official secret manifest.
lifecycle-contract.state-4: revoke: admin revoke removes runtime access for exactly one assignment and records an audit-safe event.
lifecycle-contract.state-5: reissue: admin/user-approved rotation creates a new owner payload and removes the old runtime secret.

constraints.constraint-1: No MTProxy secret is issued before email ownership verification.
constraints.constraint-2: Admin APIs, audit events, health checks, and logs must expose only redacted fingerprints, counts, assignment ids, and statuses.
constraints.constraint-3: Raw secrets may be returned only in the authenticated owner payload needed to configure Telegram.
constraints.constraint-4: Revoke and reissue must be idempotent and must not rotate unrelated users.
constraints.constraint-5: Backup/restore must preserve enough assignment metadata to reproduce or safely reissue user access after relocation.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-19: Phase-40 plan selects official per-user secrets as the access primitive because KPprotoN fake-TLS SNI policy did not pass real client-level req_pq proof.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-19: Local execution passed official dd-secret provisioning, owner API, admin revoke/reissue, manifest redaction, and assignment registry regression tests.

M-053.hotspot: H-040: Per-user MTProxy secret sync must revoke and reissue exactly one user without leaking raw secrets or breaking unrelated active users.
